remember: Students will be able to recall the types of water present in the atmosphere, their characteristics and examples.
Students will be able to identify and describe the sources of water in the atmosphere.
Students will be able to list the main causes of water evaporation and condensation.
understand: Students will be able to explain the process of water evaporation and condensation.
Students will be able to describe the role of atmosphere in the water cycle.
Students will be able to explain the concept of precipitation and its types.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concept of water cycle to explain the formation of clouds, fog, and mist.
Students will be able to use the knowledge of water in the atmosphere to explain the impact of climate change on water resources.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the causes and effects of water scarcity and droughts on the environment and human societies.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of human activities on the water cycle and water resources.

ncert_objectives: Explain the composition and characteristics of water present in the atmosphere.
Describe the water cycle and its importance.
Discuss the causes and consequences of climate change and its impact on water resources.
board_exam_objectives: Explain water cycle and its different stages (evaporation, condensation, precipitation).
Describe the role of atmosphere in water cycle and the factors affecting it.
Discuss the impact of human activities on water resources and climate change.
